findings.quote: Building a clock that kept accurate time under conditions at sea.
findings.note: The Harrison/longitude story is well known; the obstacle was the practical marine chronometer, so a knowledgeable solver picks B. Distractors are substantive (theory vs practice, lunar method), so world-knowledge answerability not a form leak.

findings.quote: The field was quick to ask whether profiting from distraction serves users.
findings.note: D and C read as reversed against how a critical attention-economy piece frames itself (the field is typically 'slow to ask'; designers speak 'without embarrassment'), so both are eliminable blind, narrowing to A/B. Partial narrowing, A vs B not decided.

findings.quote: Factors other than wolves may explain much of the recovery.
findings.note: 'Qualification' stem favors the hedged A; B ('no measurable effect') is an eliminable absolute and C (elk increased after predators returned) is counterfactual to predator dynamics. Blind narrowing plus domain knowledge, not fully decided.

findings.quote: It blocks the chemicals that produce sleepiness.
findings.note: General knowledge: caffeine blocks adenosine (the sleepiness chemical); C and D are off-topic recycled facts and B is a non-mechanism, so the key is selectable blind. World-knowledge answerability.

findings.quote: Ready to have your mind blown? Here are five amazing facts that will make you love your daily cup even more.
findings.note: class 4: register break — clickbait listicle voice with sustained direct reader address ('you probably', 'Pretty wild, huh?'), wholly out of keeping with the academic-magazine ELF register; grammatically native but wrong genre; editor's rewrite: recast as neutral expository prose

findings.quote: Ready to have your mind blown?
findings.note: class 4: register break — direct reader address and clickbait/marketing cadence alien to serious-magazine ELF expository prose; fluent native English but wrong register throughout. Editor's rewrite: recast as neutral exposition.

findings.quote: Ready to have your mind blown? Here are five amazing facts that will make you love your daily cup even more.
findings.note: class 4: register break. Fluent native English, but clickbait/marketing cadence and direct reader address are out of keeping with the academic-magazine ELF register; consistent listicle tone throughout ('Pretty wild, huh?', 'How cool is that?').

findings.quote: Smash that like button, drop a comment below, and don't you dare forget to share this
findings.note: class 4: register break — YouTube/clickbait promotional voice incompatible with the serious-magazine ELF register; grammatically native but out of genre; editor's rewrite: remove the call-to-action and recast as expository prose

findings.quote: Mind. Blown. Honestly, your friends are going to think you're some kind of plant ___(4)___ with secret powers.
findings.note: class 4: sustained clickbait/marketing register with direct reader address ('Smash that like button, drop a comment below') — fluent native English but incompatible with academic-magazine ELF register. Editor's rewrite: recast as neutral exposition.

findings.quote: Mind. Blown. Honestly, your friends are going to think you're some kind of plant ___(4)___ with secret powers.
findings.note: class 4: register break. Native-fluent but sustained clickbait/influencer cadence ('Smash that like button', 'Let's dive right in') is out of register for serious-magazine ELF expository prose.

findings.quote: The work of women appointed during the war showed that the arguments were flawed.
findings.note: C ('completely changed traditional roles') and D ('the best wartime diplomats of all') are eliminable overclaims from form alone, narrowing blind to the measured A. Partial narrowing, A vs B not decided without the passage.

findings.quote: Virtually nothing of the Pantheon erected by Agrippa remains today.
findings.note: World knowledge of the Pantheon (Hadrian's full rebuild replaced Agrippa's structure) lets one eliminate B and lean toward A, but the exact key is not decided blind. Per calibration this is the correctly-calibrated flag, routed to adjudication.

findings.quote: They involved warnings of perceived dangers at sea.
findings.note: The danger-warning function of sea-monster maps is the well-known thesis (cf. Van Duzer); domain-answerable, but A/B/D are substantive competing interpretations a passage would ground.

findings.quote: delprov elf engelsk läsförståelse what is the main
findings.note: 8-gram containment 0.0115 exceeds flag threshold 0.01 (3/260 grams shared)

findings.quote: The basic idea of Silent Spring remains controversial.
findings.note: General knowledge of Silent Spring's still-contested legacy (climate-debate parallels) leans C; A ('come to an end'), B ('industry accepted') and D ('Carson wrong') contradict the known ongoing debate. Domain-answerable, distractors substantive.

findings.quote: Kontroll över vardagens rytm och därmed över människorna.
findings.note: Stem 'ytterst handlade om' cues the abstract power/control answer; C (astronomical accuracy) and D (soltid difference) read as surface/technical detail and can be down-weighted blind, narrowing toward A. Partial blind narrowing, not decided.

findings.quote: Många arter är känsliga för föroreningar som svaveldioxid.
findings.note: General biology: lichens are classic bioindicators sensitive to SO2 and grow slowly (refuting B 'grow fast'); C (dating) and D (competition) are off-topic to air quality. Key selectable with domain knowledge, distractors substantive.

findings.quote: De markerade gränsen mot det farliga och okända.
findings.note: Chet Van Duzer is a real map scholar whose known thesis is that sea monsters marked danger and the unknown; answerable by domain knowledge, but B/C/D are substantive competing hypotheses a passage would ground.

findings.quote: Okej, så här är grejen med gamla kartor: de är egentligen ganska sjukt opålitliga.
findings.note: class 5: colloquial talshow-Swedish ('grejen', 'sjukt') breaks the edited-sakprosa register expected of a LÄS passage. native: neutral essay register

findings.quote: som har kollat på jättemånga sådana kartor
findings.note: class 5: 'kollat på' and 'jättemånga' are spoken-register colloquialisms. native: 'studerat många sådana kartor'

findings.quote: Kanske borde vi återinföra monstren, typ som en påminnelse
findings.note: class 5: filler 'typ' plus 'polare'/'coola' elsewhere sustain a slang register alien to sakprosa

findings.quote: Så nästa gång du zoomar in på Google Maps
findings.note: class 5: direct reader address ('du ... tänk på') is a register break for an exam essay text

findings.quote: Att tystnaden är en historiskt skapad norm snarare än något givet.
findings.note: Main-idea stem; C ('förbjudit allt tal') is an eliminable absolute overclaim, leaving the nuanced A against its direct negation B. Partial blind narrowing, A vs B not decided without the passage.

findings.quote: Det handlar mer om symbolik än om teknisk nödvändighet.
findings.note: Reflective 'antyder' framing favors A; the 'saknar helt känslomässiga inslag' option is A's direct negation and the 'fackförbund' option is filler, narrowing blind. Partial narrowing, not decided.

findings.quote: Pamfletten och sociala medier fyller likartade funktioner.
findings.note: Stem asks which comparison the text makes; A is the only option phrased as a parallel, and C (printing invented in Wittenberg) is false by general knowledge (Gutenberg/Mainz). Blind narrowing toward A, distractors otherwise substantive.

findings.quote: Att gemensam förvaltning kan fungera hållbart med rätt institutioner.
findings.note: This is Elinor Ostrom's Nobel-winning thesis against Hardin; B and D are opposite scholarly stances (substantive positions), so domain knowledge picks A but the passage genuinely grounds a live debate. World-knowledge answerability, not a structural leak.

findings.quote: Uppfunna traditioner uppstår ofta i tider av snabb förändring.
findings.note: Hobsbawm's 'invented traditions'; C (tartan systematized in medieval times) is false by general knowledge and D (aim to expose traditions as false) contradicts Hobsbawm's known point. Blind narrowing via domain knowledge; A and B are both defensible supported statements.

findings.quote: På grund av dåtidens tillverkningsmetod.
findings.note: The 'glass slowly flowed down' explanation (B) is a famous debunked myth and C ('glass is a liquid at room temperature') is false; a scientifically literate solver picks A. Distractors are the substantive misconception the passage exists to rebut, so world-knowledge not pure recall.

findings.quote: Etniskt splittrade samhällen.
findings.note: The argues-for vs argues-against asymmetry (unemployment/inequality/corrupt rulers all drive unrest, while ethnic fragmentation works against a unified spring) is visible only through domain reasoning, not from option form, where the four are parallel. Per calibration this is domain reasoning in a structural costume, so flag not kill.
